results.claim: GEMM rows 1-3 combined are 67.3% f_decode at BS=32, all ranked higher than GDN recurrent at rank 4
GEMM BW utilization at BS=32 is 54-65%, well below 85% threshold indicating real unoptimized headroom
Debate summary dismissed GEMM as non-optimizable citing only champion-1's single thin-GEMM experiment
Champion-2 proposal targets only GDN recurrent kernel, no GEMM micro-experiment included
Champion-3 proposal also targets only GDN recurrent, zero GEMM experiments
Champion-1 extrapolated from 4 BW-bound shapes to claim GEMM is non-optimizable for all cases
Champion-1's thin-GEMM benchmark tested only 4 shapes showing cuBLAS ahead by 14-15%
Champion-2 applied cold-cache 2.04x directly to get 7.0% E2E without production discount
Champion-2 argument labeled 5.9% as conservative and 6.9% as moderate E2E
Original proposal labeled conservative estimate as 4.6% (1.5x speedup), not 5.9%
Debate judges corrected champion-2's E2E from 4.6-6.0% to 2.8-4.6%
constraints.md states GDN recurrent state uses FP32 accumulation, leading to misinterpretation as FP32 storage
op002 validation found the BF16 state optimization is already active by default in vLLM
op002 clarified 'FP32 accumulation' in constraints.md meant compute precision, not storage dtype
Champion-2 reported cosine similarity 0.999986 at decay 0.95 using the wrong recurrence formula
Champion-1 showed the actual formula includes S@k feedback, making champion-2's precision test invalid
Running the correct formula produced 3/5 divergences at steps 157-329
Champion-2 argument claims 5.9% conservative and 6.9% moderate E2E at BS=32
Original proposal used 4.6% as conservative (1.5x) and 6.0% as moderate (1.75x)
Judges corrected range to 2.8-4.6%, rejecting champion-2's inflated figures
SMEM single-buffered at 64KB because double-buffering 128KB exceeds L40S 100KB/SM limit
SMEM CTA kernel regressed at BS=32: 0.533x warm, 0.939x cold
Best Triton config achieves only 1.034x production speedup, below 1.1x kill criterion
Kernel correctness passed: state max diff 7.15e-07 across all batch sizes
Default config resolution shows mamba_ssm_cache_dtype='auto' resolves to BF16 for BF16 models
Stage 1 baseline command used no --mamba-ssm-cache-dtype flag, so default 'auto' = BF16 was used
Benchmark confirmed FP32 vs BF16 cold speedup is 1.95x at BS=32 but already captured in baseline
Delegate-3a described champion-1 as Triton Skinny-M GEMM, not the actual CUDA C++ GDN rewrite
Delegate-3a described champion-2 as CUTLASS CuTe fused decode, not the actual BF16 state Triton modification
Champion-3's references section does not cite delegate-3a's rival analysis, confirming it was unused
Delegate-1a documented grid=(1,4,1024), BV=32, num_warps=1, state = [BS,32,128,128] FP32
Delegate-2a documented the same grid configuration, state shape, and kernel parameters
Delegate-1a provided BW floor estimates not found in bottleneck_analysis.md
Champion-1 cited delegate-1a for roofline calculation in proposal
All 9 configs converge to ~212-216us cold, confirming 73% peak BW ceiling is invariant
BV sweep shows all working configs converge to ~630 GB/s cold BW (73% peak)
Warm/cold ratio is 5.24x at BS=32 under CUDA graph capture
Campaign summary claims both GDN and GEMM are proven non-optimizable
op001 failed: SMEM budget exceeded, cold BW ceiling at 73%, best E2E 0.7%
op002 failed: optimization already deployed as vLLM default
Debate scoring rubric shows all champions provided grounded proposals with micro-experiments
Debate identified 5 key findings including cold BW ceiling, L2 lever, GEMM non-viability
Model is firmly in BW-bound regime at all batch sizes
E2E baseline measurements: 6.546s (BS=1), 7.836s (BS=8), 10.266s (BS=32)
op001 validation recommends targeting GEMM at 67.3% over GDN at 13.8%
